workflows-reference.purpose: Provide Domain-Driven Design plus SOLID plus BDD/TDD workflows for Compass tasks.

documentation-context.rule: Read docs/architecture/domain-driven-design.md and docs/architecture/bounded-context-structure.md before design or implementation.
Read docs/foundation/domain-modeling-principles.md before naming domain concepts or creating abstractions.
Read docs/process/bdd-tdd-workflow.md before selecting examples and tests.
Read relevant docs/modules notes for the bounded context or domain capability.

task-memory-gate.rule: For long or risky work with at least two concrete implementation slices after alignment or design, load Compass references/task-memory.xml.
Implementation may not start until the task memory outcome is reported as created, resumed, or not-required.
When task memory is required, docs/.tasks/<task>/goal.md, diagram.md, and memories.md must exist before the first implementation edit or command.
Missing docs/_templates task memory files are a documentation gap, not permission to skip this gate.

workflow.description: Add one business capability by clarifying language, context, examples, model, and tests before implementation.

enforcement.rule: Run phases sequentially: language discovery, context design, implementation.
Execute exactly one bounded-context behavior per workflow.
BDD examples must clarify behavior before technical TDD begins.
Do not place behavior in a context until ownership and language are explicit.
Implementation must stop after each verified domain/application/adapter checkpoint.
Before implementation starts, satisfy the task-memory-gate for any long or risky multi-slice feature.

phase.name: language-discovery
phase.purpose: Align ubiquitous language and behavior examples before designing code.

actions.action: Read DDD architecture, bounded-context structure, domain modeling, and BDD/TDD docs.
Identify the business capability and candidate bounded context.
List domain terms, definitions, ambiguous words, and rejected meanings.
Write Given/When/Then behavior examples for happy, common, and important failure paths.
Split behaviors owned by other contexts into pending work.

phase.name: context-design
phase.purpose: Design the domain model, application use case, and behavior-test matrix inside the bounded context.

actions.action: Choose the bounded context folder from docs/architecture/bounded-context-structure.md.
Define aggregate, entity, value object, domain service, domain event, and invariant candidates.
Define application use case input, output, business errors, and consumer-owned ports.
Apply SOLID at domain and application boundaries.
Map BDD examples to TDD levels: domain, application, adapter, or integration.
Mark examples selected, rejected, or postponed.

phase-output.domain-model: Aggregates, values, invariants, policies, and events.
phase-output.application-contract: Use case, inputs, outputs, errors, and ports.
phase-output.docs-impact: Context map, module note, or decision updates.

phase.name: implementation
phase.purpose: Implement approved DDD behavior through domain, application, and adapter checkpoints.

actions.action: Before the first implementation edit or command, report the task memory outcome: created, resumed, or not-required.
Start with domain behavior or application use case tests from approved examples.
Implement domain model and application orchestration before adapters unless approved otherwise.
Keep dependencies inward and interfaces consumer-owned.
Add adapters only after domain and application behavior are protected.
Stop after each checkpoint and ask before continuing.

phase-output.current-checkpoint: Domain, application, or adapter checkpoint implemented.

workflow.evidence: Approved language, approved context design, BDD examples, TDD verification, and docs update when domain ownership changes.

workflow.description: Change existing domain behavior by controlling the delta from protected bounded-context behavior.

enforcement.rule: Run phases sequentially: baseline-discovery, delta-design, implementation-checkpoints, and rollout-cleanup when rollout guards or compatibility paths are introduced.
Classify as architecture_change first when the update changes bounded-context ownership, aggregate ownership, integration contracts, or dependency direction.
Before implementation starts, satisfy the task-memory-gate for any long, risky, or multi-slice update.
Use ubiquitous language consistently and do not duplicate another context's rule locally.
High-risk updates must define rollback, feature-flag, dark-launch, compatibility, or cleanup evidence before implementation.

phase.name: baseline-discovery
phase.purpose: Protect current domain language, examples, and context ownership before deciding what may change.

actions.action: Find the owning bounded context, aggregate or use case, current ubiquitous language, examples, tests, docs, commands, and integration points.
State current domain behavior, invariants, policies, integration contracts, and expectations that must remain stable.
Classify update risk as minor, medium, or high based on blast radius, reversibility, data impact, user visibility, and context-boundary impact.
Record ambiguity before designing the delta.

phase-output.baseline: Owning context, language, behavior examples, tests, docs, and verification commands observed.
phase-output.preserve-list: Behavior, language, invariants, contracts, and non-goals that must not drift.
phase-output.risk-level: minor, medium, or high with reason.

phase.name: delta-design
phase.purpose: Define the exact domain behavior change and executable examples before editing.

actions.action: State old behavior, new behavior, unchanged behavior, and intentionally removed behavior if any.
Add or update Given/When/Then examples for preserved and changed behavior.
Map examples to domain, application, adapter, or integration tests at the cheapest sufficient level.
Define the smallest owning context, aggregate, policy, use case, or adapter to change.
Decide whether the update needs a feature flag, dark launch, compatibility path, rollback plan, monitoring, docs update, or decision record.
Define the implementation slices and verification command for each slice.

phase-output.delta: Approved behavior change and unchanged behavior.
phase-output.examples: Updated behavior examples and test mapping.
phase-output.rollout-risk: Flag, rollback, monitoring, compatibility, cleanup, or not needed.
phase-output.docs-impact: Context docs, language docs, decisions, or none.

phase.name: implementation-checkpoints
phase.purpose: Apply one approved delta slice inside the owning context.

actions.action: Before the first implementation edit or command, report the task memory outcome: created, resumed, or not-required.
Add or update the approved examples and tests for the current slice when practical.
Change the smallest owning domain model, application use case, adapter, or integration point and avoid introducing competing language.
Run the current slice verification command.
Stop after each verified slice and ask before continuing.

phase-output.baseline-protection: Example or regression evidence added or reused.

phase.name: rollout-cleanup
phase.purpose: Close temporary risk controls introduced for the update.

actions.action: Record feature flag, dark launch, compatibility path, monitoring, rollback, or cleanup status when any were introduced.
Remove stale flags or inactive compatibility paths after the update is fully accepted.
Update context docs, decisions, public contracts, or runbooks when behavior or rollout expectations changed.
Run final verification for the touched scope.

phase-output.rollout-state: Flag, rollout, compatibility, rollback, or cleanup state.

workflow.evidence: Approved baseline, approved delta design, updated examples, regression coverage for preserved behavior, changed-behavior verification, slice checkpoints, and rollout or cleanup evidence when needed.

steps.step: Reproduce the bug as a business behavior example.
Identify the bounded context and invariant or policy that failed.
Add a failing regression test at the proper DDD level.
Run affected context tests and broader integration tests when contracts changed.

workflow.evidence: Behavior example, regression test, bounded-context fix, and verification.

steps.step: Identify whether the change affects context boundaries, aggregate ownership, integration contracts, or dependency direction.
Compare 2-3 context or model options and trade-offs.
Update context map, architecture docs, or decision records before implementation.
Implement one context or integration change at a time.
Verify context tests and integration contracts.

workflow.evidence: Updated DDD docs, context/integration tests, and dependency-boundary review.
